name: semantic-code-hunter
description: Use when you need to find code by concept (not just text). Uses Serena MCP for semantic code search across the codebase with minimal token usage. Ideal for understanding architecture, finding authentication flows, or multi-file refactoring.
qualityReason: Specific MCP use with architectural focus
capabilities: Find symbols by concept instead of exact name match
Trace code relationships and dependencies across files
Get file structure overview without loading full content
Search for authentication, validation, and business logic patterns
Locate all usages of a function, class, or interface
Map complete code flows across multiple files
author: Barnhardt-Enterprises-Inc
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill with no executable code. All 28 static findings are false positives: backticks in Markdown are code formatting syntax, not shell execution; source URL is metadata; hash strings and text descriptions contain no cryptographic code or C2 indicators.
valueStatement: Finding code by exact text match misses related concepts and requires reading many files. This skill uses Serena MCP semantic search to find code by meaning, reducing token usage by 93 percent while finding more relevant results across your codebase.
userTitle: Find code by concept with semantic search
limitations: Requires Serena MCP server to be running and indexed
Not suitable for simple text searches or very small projects
Cannot edit or modify code directly
Requires understanding of semantic search concepts

useCases.title: Map system architecture
useCases.description: Understand how components connect and trace data flow across the entire codebase

useCases.title: Find authentication flows
useCases.description: Locate all authentication, authorization, and token handling code quickly

useCases.title: Refactor with confidence
useCases.description: Find every usage of a symbol before renaming or modifying shared code

promptTemplates.title: Find authentication code
promptTemplates.prompt: Use semantic-code-hunter to find all authentication-related symbols and their usages. Start with find_symbol for auth concepts, then trace the flow from login to token generation.

promptTemplates.title: Explore file structure
promptTemplates.prompt: Use semantic-code-hunter to get an overview of symbols in the services directory. Identify the main classes and functions that handle user data.

promptTemplates.title: Trace dependencies
promptTemplates.scenario: Find where a function is called
promptTemplates.prompt: Use semantic-code-hunter to find all symbols that reference the User model. Trace from creation through all usage points.

promptTemplates.title: Plan refactoring
promptTemplates.prompt: Use semantic-code-hunter to find all references to the PaymentProcessor class. List every file that needs updates before renaming.

bestPractices: Start with broad search terms and narrow down with type filters
Combine semantic search with grep for text matches and read for details
Use find_referencing_symbols to understand impact before making changes
antiPatterns: Using semantic search for simple text replacements
Skipping the overview step and jumping directly to specific symbols
Not re-indexing after adding new files to the project

faq.question: What is the difference between semantic search and grep?
faq.answer: Grep finds exact text matches. Semantic search finds code by meaning and concept, discovering related symbols even with different names.

faq.question: How much token savings does this provide?
faq.answer: Semantic search typically uses 93 percent fewer tokens compared to reading entire files and manual analysis.

faq.question: Does this work with all programming languages?
faq.answer: Serena MCP supports major languages including JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Java, Go, and Rust with more being added.

faq.question: Is my code data sent to external servers?
faq.answer: The skill is purely instructional. Code handling depends on Serena MCP configuration which runs locally or with your chosen provider.

faq.question: Why are no results returned for my search?
faq.answer: Check spelling, try broader terms, or re-index your project. Serena may need the project indexed first with serena project index.

faq.question: When should I use this instead of regular search?
faq.answer: Use semantic search for understanding architecture, finding concepts, and multi-file refactoring. Use grep for simple text matches in small projects.
